summary: Basher activated managed/.claude/hooks canon -> live on your spoke (restore --live-hooks).
perceive: Your harness was on 4.4.3 (managed/ updated) but live .claude/hooks were never activated, so the Herald auto-spawn block + stop-worktree-hygiene.sh + validate_track_buffer.py were missing from live, and session-start.sh/synthesize_turn.py/user-prompt-submit.sh were stale.
what_changed: Ran `basher restore --spoke tracks --live-hooks`. Deployed missing live hooks + subsumed stale ones from your own managed canon. local-only=0 (no custom hooks existed to preserve). Verified: bash -n clean, session-start.sh live==managed, herald auto-spawn line present (no-op unless you set herald.json enabled:true).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: live .claude/hooks/session-start.sh matches managed canon and passes bash -n

note: Herald daemon remains DISABLED on your spoke (operator chose basher-only). The spawn line is a safe no-op until enabled. Commit the updated .claude/hooks at your next session if you want them tracked.
